title: The `--userns keep-id:uid=xxx` failed when executing user's UID is very big.
body:
### Issue Description

Running rootless podman with `--userns keep-id:uid=1000` failed if it's executed by user with high UID.

```shell
$ podman version
Client:        Podman Engine
Version:       5.6.0
API Version:   5.6.0
Go Version:    go1.24.6 (Red Hat 1.24.6-1.el10)
Git Commit:    56f1962fb0af60c29c7c5d27bdb4f26b13e69afe
Built:         Fri Aug 22 00:00:00 2025
Build Origin:  builder@centos.org
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64

$ uname -a
Linux rhp00rduvwlnx 6.12.0-161.el10.x86_64 #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Thu Nov 20 12:23:19 UTC 2025 x86_64 GNU/Linux

$ cat /etc/os-release
NAME="CentOS Stream"
VERSION="10 (Coughlan)"
ID="centos"
ID_LIKE="rhel fedora"
VERSION_ID="10"
PLATFORM_ID="platform:el10"
PRETTY_NAME="CentOS Stream 10 (Coughlan)"
ANSI_COLOR="0;31"
LOGO="fedora-logo-icon"
CPE_NAME="cpe:/o:centos:centos:10"
HOME_URL="https://centos.org/"
VENDOR_NAME="CentOS"
VENDOR_URL="https://centos.org/"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://issues.redhat.com/"
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT="Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10"
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT_VERSION="CentOS Stream"

```


### Steps to reproduce the issue

```shell
$ # Running as `etirtara`.
$ id
uid=1000000(etirtara) gid=10000(rhtusers) groups=10000(rhtusers),4(adm),988(docker) context=unconfined_u:unconfined_r:unconfined_t:s0-s0:c0.c1023

$ podman run --rm -it --user 1000:0 --userns keep-id:uid=1000 alpine:latest sh
Error: crun: open `/home/etirtara/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/ee0611a95f08e0e88088ab265d92c853396a6db88ed7977d55cd3b4b232d4598/merged`: Permission denied: OCI permission denied

$ podman --log-level=debug run --rm -it --user 1000:0 --userns keep-id:uid=1000 alpine:latest sh 2>&1 | grep -Eai "error|fail|uid|map|namespace"
time="2025-12-06T17:29:29Z" level=debug msg="Called run.PersistentPreRunE(podman --log-level=debug run --rm -it --user 1000:0 --userns keep-id:uid=1000 alpine:latest sh)"
time="2025-12-06T17:29:29Z" level=debug msg="Configured OCI runtime youki initialization failed: no valid executable found for OCI runtime youki: invalid argument"
time="2025-12-06T17:29:29Z" level=debug msg="Configured OCI runtime krun initialization failed: no valid executable found for OCI runtime krun: invalid argument"
time="2025-12-06T17:29:29Z" level=debug msg="Configured OCI runtime crun-vm initialization failed: no valid executable found for OCI runtime crun-vm: invalid argument"
time="2025-12-06T17:29:29Z" level=debug msg="Configured OCI runtime runc initialization failed: no valid executable found for OCI runtime runc: invalid argument"
time="2025-12-06T17:29:29Z" level=debug msg="Configured OCI runtime ocijail initialization failed: no valid executable found for OCI runtime ocijail: invalid argument"
time="2025-12-06T17:29:29Z" level=debug msg="Configured OCI runtime crun-wasm initialization failed: no valid executable found for OCI runtime crun-wasm: invalid argument"
time="2025-12-06T17:29:29Z" level=debug msg="Configured OCI runtime runj initialization failed: no valid executable found for OCI runtime runj: invalid argument"
time="2025-12-06T17:29:29Z" level=debug msg="Configured OCI runtime kata initialization failed: no valid executable found for OCI runtime kata: invalid argument"
time="2025-12-06T17:29:29Z" level=debug msg="Configured OCI runtime runsc initialization failed: no valid executable found for OCI runtime runsc: invalid argument"
time="2025-12-06T17:29:29Z" level=debug msg="Cached value indicated that idmapped mounts for overlay are not supported"
time="2025-12-06T17:29:29Z" level=debug msg="Check for idmapped mounts support "
time="2025-12-06T17:29:29Z" level=debug msg="ExitCode msg: \"container create failed (no logs from conmon): conmon bytes \\\"\\\": readobjectstart: expect { or n, but found \\x00, error found in #0 byte of ...||..., bigger context ...||...\""
Error: container create failed (no logs from conmon): conmon bytes "": readObjectStart: expect { or n, but found , error found in #0 byte of ...||..., bigger context ...||...

$ # Running as `lcl-adm`.
$ id
uid=1000(lcl-adm) gid=1000(lcl-adm) groups=1000(lcl-adm),4(adm),10(wheel) context=unconfined_u:unconfined_r:unconfined_t:s0-s0:c0.c1023

$ podman run --rm -it --user 1000:0 --userns keep-id:uid=1000 alpine:latest sh
Resolved "alpine" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull docker.io/library/alpine:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 014e56e61396 done   |
Copying config 7acffee03f done   |
Writing manifest to image destination
~ $ id
uid=1000(lcl-adm) gid=0(root) groups=1000,0(root)
~ $

```

I tested with different uid/gig subordinate (alternate with the ones got commented out):
```shell
$  cat /etc/subuid
#lcl-adm:524288:65536
lcl-adm:10000001:999
lcl-adm:10001001:64536
#etirtara:1048576:65536
etirtara:10000001:65536

$  cat /etc/subgid
#lcl-adm:524288:65536
lcl-adm:10000001:999
lcl-adm:10001001:64536
#etirtara:1048576:65536
```



### Describe the results you received

It seems if the executing user's UID is > 65535, it failed.

### Describe the results you expected

Since linux support UID > 65535, then it should work when the executing user's UID above 65535.

### podman info output

```yaml

  kernel: 6.12.0-161.el10.x86_64

    package: netavark-1.16.0-1.el10.x86_64

  ociRuntime:
    name: crun

    path: /usr/bin/crun
    version: |-
      crun version 1.24
      commit: 54693209039e5e04cbe3c8b1cd5fe2301219f0a1
      rundir: /run/user/1000000/crun
      spec: 1.0.0
      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +CRIU +YAJL
  os: linux
  pasta:
    executable: /usr/bin/pasta
    package: passt-0^20250512.g8ec1341-2.el10.x86_64

  graphOptions: {}
  graphRoot: /home/etirtara/.local/share/containers/storage
  graphRootAllocated: 16792809472

    Backing Filesystem: extfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "true"
    Supports d_type: "true"
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
